function_reachability: Is there a call path from function 'main' to function 'foo'?

Is it impossible for function '__builtin_abort' to reach function 'foo'?
loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'i < N' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i < N' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i < N' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'i < N' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 14:  <statement> int </statement> is reached, the statement line 24:  <statement> if (out[i] != in[i]) </statement> has already been reached?
Does the statement line 15:  <statement> main (void) </statement> dominate line 33:  <statement> __builtin_abort (); </statement>?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable out at line 9: <statement>       out += step; </statement> depend on the value of variable step at line 3: <statement> foo (int *out, int *in, int step) </statement>?
Does variable in at line 3: <statement> foo (int *out, int *in, int step) </statement> determine the value stored in variable i at line 5: <statement>   for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'i (line 5)' remain in use at the end of function 'foo'?
Does variable 'step' remain in use at the end of function 'foo'?
dead_code: Can the statement 'if (out[i] != in[i])' be considered dead code?
